premise: When my health declined at 54, my adult son and his wife begged for medical power of attorney, promising to care for me at home; instead they placed me in a cheap state facility, sold my paid‑off $210,000 condo, and used the cash to buy a deluxe RV. I befriended a nurse who helped me document their neglect, petitioned to regain my estate, won a $250,000 elder‑exploitation judgment, and accepted the RV as partial payment — now I live in it full‑time, traveling to national parks and blogging about 'Golden Years Reclaimed,' with their names permanently redacted.

thumb.label: Thumbnail + metadata
thumb.detail: My Son Stole My Home And Dumped Me In A Facility. So I Accepted His RV And Now I
